title: Array Methods You'll Actually Use
subtitle: push, pop, map, filter — the workhorses

sections.heading: JavaScript gives you tools
sections.body: JavaScript arrays come with built-in methods that handle the most common operations for you. Learning these is half of writing fluent JS.

sections.heading: Transform with map / filter / reduce
sections.body:
These three methods let you transform arrays without writing loops by hand.

• **`map`** — make a new array where each element is transformed.
• **`filter`** — make a new array keeping only elements that pass a test.
• **`reduce`** — boil the whole array down to a single value.

sections.prompt: Write a function `sumPositive(arr)` that returns the sum of all positive numbers in the array.
hints: Use filter() to keep only positive numbers.
Then use reduce() to sum them up, starting from 0.

questions.question: Which method REMOVES the last element of an array?
questions.explanation: pop() removes and returns the last element. push() adds to the end.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: map applies the function to each element. 1+10=11, 2+10=12, 3+10=13.
